criteria.tier1: High virality, clear false/misleading signal, public impact
criteria.tier2: Medium virality or partially confirmed, needs verification
criteria.tier3: Low virality, confirmed/uncontroversial, or administrative data

rankings.reason: AI-generated fake video about sitting PM — high virality, political impact
Hantavirus conspiracy targeting active public health event — dangerous health misinformation
Vaccine-hantavirus link claim — dangerous medical misinformation during active outbreak
Prophecy-as-proof claim — classic conspiracy pattern, outbreak context amplifies danger
AI deepfake audio of serving US President — severe political misinformation
OpenAI smartphone — major tech rumor with partial analyst confirmation, needs verification
Amazon smartphone — rumor officially denied by executive
AI-generated images of geopolitical event — blurs real/fake boundary
Fake ICE shooting images — sensitive immigration topic, AI manipulation
Digitally altered photo of public figure — fact-checked by Snopes
Trump-China state visit — partially confirmed diplomatic activity
Florida joyriding ban — under active fact-check
Obama coup claim — recurring conspiracy, renewed by Truth Social posts
2020 election fraud — perennial claim, newly amplified
MMR-autism claim — resurfaced by influencers
Hantavirus etymology misinfo — medium virality, health context
Hantavirus Hebrew etymology (duplicate angle) — same as M027
Sichuan accident rumor — regional impact, verified by Chinese authorities
Henan fire old news re-circulated — pattern of reposting old disasters
Bridge project rumor — local interest, regulatory action taken
Trump 55 posts — confirmed behavior, claims within vary
Deepfake statistics — legitimate data claim worth noting
RAM shortage impact — confirmed by official source
Truck accident rumor — low virality, local
Arrest claim — single person, local news
Samsung official announcement — not misinformation
AI phone hyperbole — media speculation, low harm
MIT study citation — legitimate research data
